mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean or Rank IC exceeds 0.02 when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and real GDP confirms robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and GDPC1 yields negative mean 60-day returns or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which correlates with higher corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of greater than 1% coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 8.0%, concurrent increases in the federal funds rate force aggressive portfolio de-risking, producing sharp negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and consumer spending power, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth exceeding 100k yields negative mean 60-day returns or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances following a greater than 5% HOUST drop.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and expansion in the unemployment rate confirms a broad-based economic slowdown, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a win rate above 50% or loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.
